stimulus: “The Story of an Hour” is an 1894 short story by Kate Chopin. In the story, the protagonist, Louise Mallard, expresses contradictory feelings about her grief: ______
stem: Which quotation from “The Story of an Hour” most effectively illustrates the claim?

answerOptions.content: “There were patches of blue sky showing here and there through the clouds that had met and piled one above the other in the west facing her window.”

“She knew that she would weep again when she saw the kind, tender hands folded in death; the face that had never looked save with love upon her, fixed and gray and dead. But she saw beyond that bitter moment a long procession of years to come that would belong to her absolutely.”
“Her pulses beat fast, and the coursing blood warmed and relaxed every inch of her body.”
“She did not hear the story as many women have heard the same, with a paralyzed inability to accept its significance.”

rationale: Choice B is the best answer because it directly captures Louise’s contradictory feelings: she anticipates weeping in grief at the sight of her husband, yet also envisions a long stretch of years that will belong to her — sorrow alongside anticipation, the very contradiction the claim names. Choice A is incorrect because it describes the sky, not Louise’s feelings. Choice C is incorrect because it describes a physical sensation without contradiction. Choice D is incorrect because it indicates only one feeling — her unhesitant acceptance of the news.
